NAME

audio.c - 🔊 Audio capture and playback using PortAudio with buffer management

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WORKER_BATCH_FRAMES 128
#define WORKER_BATCH_SAMPLES (WORKER_BATCH_FRAMES * AUDIO_CHANNELS)
#define WORKER_TIMEOUT_MS 1

asciichat_error_t audio_write_samples (audio_context_t *ctx, const float *buffer, int num_samples)

asciichat_error_t audio_list_output_devices (audio_device_info_t **out_devices, unsigned int *out_count)

asciichat_error_t audio_dequantize_samples (const uint8_t * samples_ptr, uint32_t total_samples, float * out_samples)

Definition at line 1978 of file lib/audio/audio.c.

1978                                                                                                                    {
1979   if (!samples_ptr || !out_samples || total_samples == 0) {
1980     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for audio dequantization");
1981   }
1982 
1983   for (uint32_t i = 0; i < total_samples; i++) {
1984     uint32_t network_sample;
1985     // Use memcpy to safely handle potential misalignment from packet header
1986     memcpy(&network_sample, samples_ptr + i * sizeof(uint32_t), sizeof(uint32_t));
1987     int32_t scaled = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(network_sample);
1988     out_samples[i] = (float)scaled / 2147483647.0f;
1989   }
1990 
1991   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1992 }

void
  audio_destroy (audio_context_t * ctx)


Definition at line 1288 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1288                                          {
1289   if (!ctx) {
1290     return;
1291   }
1292 
1293   // Always release PortAudio refcount if it was incremented
1294   // audio_init() calls Pa_Initialize() very early, and if it fails partway through,
1295   // ctx->initialized will be false. But we MUST still call audio_release_portaudio()
1296   // to properly decrement the refcount and allow Pa_Terminate() to be called.
1297   if (ctx->initialized) {
1298 
1299     // Stop duplex stream if running (this also stops the worker thread)
1300     if (ctx->running) {
1301       audio_stop_duplex(ctx);
1302     }
1303 
1304     // Ensure worker thread is stopped even if streams weren't running
1305     if (ctx->worker_running) {
1306       log_debug("Stopping worker thread during audio_destroy");
1307       atomic_store_bool(&ctx->worker_should_stop, true);
1308       cond_signal(&ctx->worker_cond); // Wake up worker if waiting

1310       ctx->worker_running = false;
1311     }
1312 
1313     mutex_lock(&ctx->state_mutex);

1316     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->capture_buffer);

1319     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->raw_render_rb);
1320     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->processed_playback_rb);
1321     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer); // May be NULL, that's OK
1322 
1323     // Free pre-allocated worker buffers
1324     SAFE_FREE(ctx->worker_capture_batch);
1325     SAFE_FREE(ctx->worker_render_batch);
1326     SAFE_FREE(ctx->worker_playback_batch);
1327

1330     mutex_destroy(&ctx->worker_mutex);
1331 
1332     ctx->initialized = false;
1333 
1334     mutex_unlock(&ctx->state_mutex);

1336 
1337     log_debug("Audio system cleanup complete (all resources released)");

1339   }

1267   /* Register audio context with named registry */
1268   NAMED_REGISTER(ctx, "audio_context", "audio_context_t", "0x%tx", NULL);
1269 
1270   /* Register audio context's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
1271   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&ctx->state_mutex, "state_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1272   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&ctx->worker_mutex, "worker_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));

1279   NAMED_REGISTER_AUDIO_RINGBUF(ctx->playback_buffer, "playback_buffer", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));

bool audio_is_supported_sample_rate (uint32_t
  sample_rate)


Definition at line 2074 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2074                                                           {
2075   // List of commonly supported audio sample rates
2076   static const uint32_t supported_rates[] = {
2077       8000,   // Telephone quality
2078       16000,  // Wideband telephony
2079       24000,  // High quality speech
2080       32000,  // Good for video
2081       44100,  // CD quality (less common in VoIP)
2082       48000,  // Standard professional
2083       96000,  // High-end professional
2084       192000, // Ultra-high-end mastering
2085   };
2086 
2087   const size_t rate_count = sizeof(supported_rates) / sizeof(supported_rates[0]);
2088   for (size_t i = 0; i < rate_count; i++) {
2089     if (sample_rate == supported_rates[i]) {
2090       return true;
2091     }
2092   }
2093 
2094   return false;
2095 }

847                                                       {
848   if (!rb)
849     return;
850 
851   mutex_lock(&rb->mutex);
852   // Reset buffer to empty state (no audio to play = silence at shutdown)
853   atomic_store_u64(&rb->write_index, 0);
854   atomic_store_u64(&rb->read_index, 0);
855   rb->last_sample = 0.0f;
856   // Clear the actual data to zeros to prevent any stale audio

size_t
  audio_ring_buffer_peek (audio_ring_buffer_t * rb, float * data, size_t
  samples)


Peek at available samples without consuming them (for AEC3 render
    signal). This function reads samples from the jitter buffer WITHOUT
    advancing the read_index. Used to feed audio to AEC3 for echo cancellation
    even during jitter buffer fill period.




Parameters


rb Ring buffer to peek from


data Output buffer for samples


samples Number of samples to peek


Returns


Number of samples actually peeked (may be less than
  requested)


Definition at line 1074 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1074                                                                                     {
1075   if (!rb || !data || samples <= 0) {
1076     return 0;
1077   }
1078 
1079   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
1080   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
1081   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
1082 
1083   // Calculate available samples
1084   size_t available;
1085   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
1086     available = write_idx - read_idx;
1087   } else {
1088     available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx + write_idx;
1089   }
1090 
1091   size_t to_peek = (samples > available) ? available : samples;
1092 
1093   if (to_peek == 0) {
1094     return 0;
1095   }
1096 
1097   // Copy samples in chunks (handle wraparound)
1098   size_t first_chunk = (read_idx + to_peek <= AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE) ? to_peek : (AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx);
1099 
1100   SAFE_MEMCPY(data, first_chunk * sizeof(float), rb->data + read_idx, first_chunk * sizeof(float));
1101 
1102   if (first_chunk < to_peek) {
1103     // Wraparound: copy second chunk from beginning of buffer
1104     size_t second_chunk = to_peek - first_chunk;
1105     SAFE_MEMCPY(data + first_chunk, second_chunk * sizeof(float), rb->data, second_chunk * sizeof(float));
1106   }
1107 
1108   return to_peek;
1109 }

  audio_ring_buffer_read (audio_ring_buffer_t * rb, float * data, size_t
  samples)

944                                                                                     {
945   if (!rb || !data || samples <= 0) {
946     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: rb=%p, data=%p, samples=%d", rb, data, samples);
947     return 0; // Return 0 samples read on error
948   }
949 
950   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
951   // - Load writer's write_index with acquire (see writer's data updates)
952   // - Load our own read_index with relaxed (no sync needed with ourselves)
953   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
954   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
955 
956   // Calculate available samples
957   size_t available;
958   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
959     available = write_idx - read_idx;
960   } else {
961     available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx + write_idx;
962   }
963 
964   // LOCK-FREE: Load jitter buffer state with acquire ordering
965   bool jitter_filled = atomic_load_u64(&rb->jitter_buffer_filled);
966   int crossfade_remaining = atomic_load_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining);
967   bool fade_in = atomic_load_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in);
968 
969   // Jitter buffer: don't read until initial fill threshold is reached
970   // (only for playback buffers - capture buffers have jitter_buffer_enabled = false)
971   if (!jitter_filled && rb->jitter_buffer_enabled) {
972     // Check if we've accumulated enough samples to start playback
973     if (available >= AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD) {
974       atomic_store_u64(&rb->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
975       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining, AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES);
976       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in, true);
977       log_info("Jitter buffer filled (%zu samples), starting playback with fade-in", available);
978       // Reload state for processing below
979       jitter_filled = true;
980       crossfade_remaining = AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES;
981       fade_in = true;
982     } else {
983       // Log buffer fill progress every second
984       log_debug_every(NS_PER_MS_INT, "Jitter buffer filling: %zu/%d samples (%.1f%%)", available,
985                       AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD, (100.0f * available) / AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD);
986       return 0; // Return 0 samples - caller will pad with silence
987     }
988   }
989 
990   // Periodic buffer health logging (every 5 seconds when healthy)
991   unsigned int underruns = atomic_load_u64(&rb->underrun_count);
992   log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Buffer health: %zu/%d samples (%.1f%%), underruns=%u", available,
993                 AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE, (100.0f * available) / AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE, underruns);
994 
995   // Low buffer handling: DON'T pause playback - continue reading what's available
996   // and fill the rest with silence. Pausing causes a feedback loop where:
997   // 1. Underrun -> pause reading -> buffer overflows from incoming samples
998   // 2. Threshold reached -> resume reading -> drains too fast -> underrun again
999   //
1000   // Instead: always consume samples to prevent overflow, use silence for missing data
1001   if (rb->jitter_buffer_enabled && available < AUDIO_JITTER_LOW_WATER_MARK) {
1002     unsigned int underrun_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&rb->underrun_count, 1) + 1;
1003     log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST,
1004                    "Audio buffer low #%u: only %zu samples available (low water mark: %d), padding with silence",
1005                    underrun_count, available, AUDIO_JITTER_LOW_WATER_MARK);
1006     // Don't set jitter_buffer_filled = false - keep reading to prevent overflow
1007   }
1008 
1009   size_t to_read = (samples > available) ? available : samples;
1010 
1011   // Optimize: copy in chunks instead of one sample at a time
1012   size_t remaining = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx;
1013 
1014   if (to_read <= remaining) {
1015     // Can copy in one chunk
1016     SAFE_MEMCPY(data, to_read * sizeof(float), &rb->data[read_idx], to_read * sizeof(float));
1017   } else {
1018     // Need to wrap around - copy in two chunks
1019     SAFE_MEMCPY(data, remaining * sizeof(float), &rb->data[read_idx], remaining * sizeof(float));
1020     SAFE_MEMCPY(&data[remaining], (to_read - remaining) * sizeof(float), &rb->data[0],
1021                 (to_read - remaining) * sizeof(float));
1022   }
1023 
1024   // LOCK-FREE: Store new read_index with release ordering
1025   // This ensures all data reads above complete before the index update
1026   unsigned int new_read_idx = (read_idx + (unsigned int)to_read) % AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE;
1027   atomic_store_u64(&rb->read_index, new_read_idx);
1028 
1029   // Apply fade-in if recovering from underrun
1030   if (fade_in && crossfade_remaining > 0) {
1031     int fade_start = AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES - crossfade_remaining;
1032     size_t fade_samples = (to_read < (size_t)crossfade_remaining) ? to_read : (size_t)crossfade_remaining;
1033 
1034     for (size_t i = 0; i < fade_samples; i++) {
1035       float fade_factor = (float)(fade_start + (int)i + 1) / (float)AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES;
1036       data[i] *= fade_factor;
1037     }
1038 
1039     int new_crossfade_remaining = crossfade_remaining - (int)fade_samples;
1040     atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining, new_crossfade_remaining);
1041     if (new_crossfade_remaining <= 0) {
1042       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in, false);
1043       log_debug("Audio fade-in complete");
1044     }
1045   }
1046 
1047   // Save last sample for potential fade-out
1048   // Note: only update if we actually read some data
1049   // This is NOT atomic - only the reader thread writes this
1050   if (to_read > 0) {
1051     rb->last_sample = data[to_read - 1];
1052   }
1053 
1054   // Return ACTUAL number of samples read, not padded count
1055   // The caller (mixer) expects truthful return values to detect underruns
1056   // and handle silence padding externally. Internal padding creates double-padding bugs.
1057   //
1058   // This function was incorrectly returning `samples` even when
1059   // it only read `to_read` samples. This broke the mixer's underrun detection.
1060   return to_read;
1061 }

861                                                                                                    {
862   if (!rb || !data || samples <= 0)
863     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: rb=%p, data=%p, samples=%d", rb, data, samples);
864 
865   // Validate samples doesn't exceed our buffer size
866   if (samples > AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE) {
867     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER, "Attempted to write %d samples, but buffer size is only %d", samples,
868                      AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE);
869   }
870 
871   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
872   // - Load our own write_index with relaxed (no sync needed with ourselves)
873   // - Load reader's read_index with acquire (see reader's updates to free space)
874   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
875   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
876 
877   // Calculate current buffer level (how many samples are buffered)
878   int buffer_level;
879   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
880     buffer_level = (int)(write_idx - read_idx);
881   } else {
882     buffer_level = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - (int)(read_idx - write_idx);
883   }
884   // Reserve 1 slot to distinguish between full and empty states
885   // (when buffer is full, write_idx will be just before read_idx, not equal to it)
886   int available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - 1 - buffer_level;
887 
888   // HIGH WATER MARK: Drop INCOMING samples to prevent latency accumulation
889   // Writer must not modify read_index (race condition with reader).
890   // Instead, we drop incoming samples to keep buffer bounded.
891   // This sacrifices newest data to prevent unbounded latency growth.
892   if (buffer_level > AUDIO_JITTER_HIGH_WATER_MARK) {
893     // Buffer is already too full - drop incoming samples to maintain target level
894     int target_writes = AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL - buffer_level;
895     if (target_writes < 0) {
896       target_writes = 0; // Buffer is way over - drop everything
897     }
898 
899     if (samples > target_writes) {
900       int dropped = samples - target_writes;
901       log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST,
902                      "Audio buffer high water mark exceeded (%d > %d): dropping %d INCOMING samples "
903                      "(keeping newest %d to maintain target %d)",
904                      buffer_level, AUDIO_JITTER_HIGH_WATER_MARK, dropped, target_writes, AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL);
905       samples = target_writes; // Only write what fits within target level
906     }
907   }
908 
909   // Now write the new samples - should always have enough space after above
910   int samples_to_write = samples;
911   if (samples > available) {
912     // This should rarely happen after the high water mark logic above
913     int samples_dropped = samples - available;
914     samples_to_write = available;
915     log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST, "Audio buffer overflow: dropping %d of %d incoming samples (buffer_used=%d/%d)",
916                    samples_dropped, samples, AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - available, AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE);
917   }
918 
919   // Write only the samples that fit (preserves existing data integrity)
920   if (samples_to_write > 0) {
921     int remaining = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - (int)write_idx;
922 
923     if (samples_to_write <= remaining) {
924       // Can copy in one chunk
925       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[write_idx], samples_to_write * sizeof(float), data, samples_to_write * sizeof(float));
926     } else {
927       // Need to wrap around - copy in two chunks
928       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[write_idx], remaining * sizeof(float), data, remaining * sizeof(float));
929       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[0], (samples_to_write - remaining) * sizeof(float), &data[remaining],
930                   (samples_to_write - remaining) * sizeof(float));
931     }
932 
933     // LOCK-FREE: Store new write_index with release ordering
934     // This ensures all data writes above are visible before the index update
935     unsigned int new_write_idx = (write_idx + (unsigned int)samples_to_write) % AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE;
936     atomic_store_u64(&rb->write_index, new_write_idx);
937   }
938 
939   // Note: jitter buffer fill check is now done in read function for better control
940 
941   return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Success

1444     log_info("  Input:  %s (%.0f Hz)", inputInfo->name, inputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1445   } else if (ctx->playback_only) {

1447   } else {
1448     log_debug("  Input:  (none)");
1449   }
1450   if (has_output) {
1451     log_info("  Output: %s (%.0f Hz)", outputInfo->name, outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1452   } else {
1453     log_debug("  Output: None (input-only mode - will send audio to server)");
1454   }
1455 
1456   // Check if sample rates differ - ALSA full-duplex doesn't handle this well
1457   // If no input or no output, always use separate streams
1458   bool rates_differ = has_input && has_output && (inputInfo->defaultSampleRate != outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1459   bool try_separate = rates_differ || !has_input || !has_output;
1460   PaError err = paNoError;
1461 
1462   if (!try_separate) {
1463     // Try full-duplex first (preferred - perfect AEC3 timing)
1464     LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1465       err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->duplex_stream, &inputParams, &outputParams, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER,
1466                           paClipOff, duplex_callback, ctx);
1467     });
1468 
1469     if (err == paNoError) {
1470       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1471         err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1472       });
1473       if (err != paNoError) {
1474         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1475           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1476         });
1477         ctx->duplex_stream = NULL;
1478         log_warn("Full-duplex stream failed to start: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1479         try_separate = true;
1480       }
1481     } else {
1482       log_warn("Full-duplex stream failed to open: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1483       try_separate = true;
1484     }
1485   }
1486 
1487   if (try_separate) {
1488     // Fall back to separate streams (needed when sample rates differ or input-only/playback-only mode)
1489     if (has_output && has_input) {
1490       log_info("Using separate input/output streams (sample rates differ: %.0f vs %.0f Hz)",
1491                inputInfo->defaultSampleRate, outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1492       log_info("  Will resample: buffer at %.0f Hz → output at %.0f Hz", (double)AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE,
1493                outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1494     } else if (has_output) {
1495       log_debug("Using output-only mode (playback-only for mirror/media)");
1496     } else if (has_input) {
1497       log_info("Using input-only mode (no output device available)");
1498     }
1499 
1500     // Store the internal sample rate (buffer rate)
1501     ctx->sample_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1502 
1503     // Create render buffer for AEC3 reference synchronization
1504     ctx->render_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1505     if (!ctx->render_buffer) {
1506       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create render buffer");
1507     }
1508 
1509     // Open output stream only if output device exists
1510     bool output_ok = false;
1511     double actual_output_rate = 0;
1512     if (has_output) {
1513       // Try to use AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE (48kHz) first for best quality and duplex compatibility
1514       // Fall back to native rate if 48kHz not supported
1515       double preferred_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1516       double native_rate = outputInfo->defaultSampleRate;
1517 
1518       log_debug("Attempting output at %.0f Hz (preferred) vs %.0f Hz (native)", preferred_rate, native_rate);
1519 
1520       // Always use output_callback for output streams (both output-only and duplex modes)
1521       // PortAudio will invoke the callback whenever it needs audio data
1522       // The callback reads from media_source (mirror mode) or playback buffers (network mode)
1523       PaStreamCallback *callback = output_callback;
1524 
1525       // Try preferred rate first
1526       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1527         err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->output_stream, NULL, &outputParams, preferred_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1528                             callback, ctx);
1529       });
1530 
1531       if (err == paNoError) {
1532         actual_output_rate = preferred_rate;
1533         output_ok = true;
1534         log_info("✓ Output opened at preferred rate: %.0f Hz (matches input - optimal!)", preferred_rate);
1535       } else {
1536         log_warn("Failed to open output at %.0f Hz: %s, trying native rate %.0f Hz", preferred_rate,
1537                  Pa_GetErrorText(err), native_rate);
1538 
1539         // If first Pa_OpenStream call left a partial stream, clean it up before retrying
1540         if (ctx->output_stream) {
1541           log_debug("Closing partially-opened output stream from failed preferred rate");
1542           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1543             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1544           });
1545           ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1546         }
1547 
1548         // Fall back to native rate (still using blocking mode for output-only)
1549         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1550           err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->output_stream, NULL, &outputParams, native_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1551                               callback, ctx);
1552         });
1553 
1554         if (err == paNoError) {
1555           actual_output_rate = native_rate;
1556           output_ok = true;
1557           log_info("✓ Output opened at native rate: %.0f Hz (will need resampling)", native_rate);
1558         } else {
1559           log_warn("Failed to open output stream at native rate: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1560           // Clean up if fallback also failed
1561           if (ctx->output_stream) {
1562             log_debug("Closing partially-opened output stream from failed native rate");
1563             LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1564               Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1565             });
1566             ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1567           }
1568         }
1569       }
1570 
1571       // Store actual output rate for resampling
1572       if (output_ok) {
1573         ctx->output_device_rate = actual_output_rate;
1574         if (actual_output_rate != AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE) {
1575           log_warn("⚠️  Output rate mismatch: %.0f Hz output vs %.0f Hz input - resampling will be used",
1576                    actual_output_rate, (double)AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
1577         }
1578       }
1579     }
1580 
1581     // Open input stream only if we have input (skip for playback-only mode)
1582     bool input_ok = !has_input; // If no input, mark as OK (skip)
1583     if (has_input) {
1584       // Use pipeline sample rate (AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE)
1585       // In input-only mode, we don't need to match output device rate
1586       double input_stream_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1587       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1588         err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->input_stream, &inputParams, NULL, input_stream_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1589                             input_callback, ctx);
1590       });
1591       input_ok = (err == paNoError);
1592 
1593       // If input failed, try device 0 as fallback (HDMI on BeaglePlay)
1594       if (!input_ok) {
1595         log_debug("Input failed - trying device 0 as fallback");
1596 
1597         // Clean up partial stream from first attempt before retrying
1598         if (ctx->input_stream) {
1599           log_debug("Closing partially-opened input stream from failed primary device");
1600           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1601             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1602           });
1603           ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1604         }
1605 
1606         PaStreamParameters fallback_input_params = inputParams;
1607         fallback_input_params.device = 0;
1608         const PaDeviceInfo *device_0_info = Pa_GetDeviceInfo(0);
1609         if (device_0_info && device_0_info->maxInputChannels > 0) {
1610           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1611             err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->input_stream, &fallback_input_params, NULL, input_stream_rate,
1612                                 AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff, input_callback, ctx);
1613           });
1614           if (err == paNoError) {
1615             log_info("Input stream opened on device 0 (fallback from default)");
1616             input_ok = true;
1617           } else {
1618             log_warn("Fallback also failed on device 0: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1619             // Clean up if fallback also failed
1620             if (ctx->input_stream) {
1621               log_debug("Closing partially-opened input stream from failed fallback device");
1622               LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1623                 Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1624               });
1625               ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1626             }
1627           }
1628         }
1629       }
1630 
1631       if (!input_ok) {
1632         log_warn("Failed to open input stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1633       }
1634     }
1635 
1636     // Check if we got at least one stream working
1637     if (!input_ok && !output_ok) {
1638       // Neither stream works - fail completely
1639       audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1640       ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1641       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to open both input and output streams");
1642     }
1643 
1644     // If output failed but input works, we can still send audio to server
1645     if (!output_ok && input_ok) {
1646       log_info("Output stream unavailable - continuing with input-only (can send audio to server)");
1647       ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1648     }
1649     // If input failed but output works, we can still receive audio from server
1650     if (!input_ok && output_ok) {
1651       log_info("Input stream unavailable - continuing with output-only (can receive audio from server)");
1652       ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1653     }
1654 
1655     // Start output stream if it's open
1656     if (ctx->output_stream) {
1657       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1658         err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->output_stream);
1659       });
1660       if (err != paNoError) {
1661         if (ctx->input_stream) {
1662           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1663             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1664           });
1665         }
1666         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1667           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1668         });
1669         ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1670         ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1671         audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1672         ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1673         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to start output stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1674       }
1675     }
1676 
1677     // Start input stream if it's open
1678     if (ctx->input_stream) {
1679       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1680         err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->input_stream);
1681       });
1682       if (err != paNoError) {
1683         if (ctx->output_stream) {
1684           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1685             Pa_StopStream(ctx->output_stream);
1686           });
1687         }
1688         if (ctx->input_stream) {
1689           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1690             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1691           });
1692         }
1693         if (ctx->output_stream) {
1694           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1695             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1696           });
1697         }
1698         ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1699         ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1700         audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1701         ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1702         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to start input stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1703       }
1704     }
1705 
1706     ctx->separate_streams = true;
1707     log_debug("Separate streams started successfully");
1708   } else {
1709     ctx->separate_streams = false;
1710     log_info("Full-duplex stream started (single callback, perfect AEC3 timing)");
1711   }
1712 
1713   audio_set_realtime_priority();
1714 
1715   // Start worker thread for heavy audio processing
1716   if (!ctx->worker_running) {
1717     atomic_store_bool(&ctx->worker_should_stop, false);
1718     if (asciichat_thread_create(&ctx->worker_thread, "audio_worker", audio_worker_thread, ctx) != 0) {
1719       // Failed to create worker thread - stop streams and cleanup
1720       if (ctx->duplex_stream) {
1721         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1722           Pa_StopStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1723           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1724         });
1725         ctx->duplex_stream = NULL;
1726       }
1727       if (ctx->input_stream) {
1728         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1729           Pa_StopStream(ctx->input_stream);
1730           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1731         });
1732         ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1733       }
1734       if (ctx->output_stream) {
1735         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1736           Pa_StopStream(ctx->output_stream);
1737           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1738         });
1739         ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1740       }
1741       audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1742       ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1743       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create worker thread");
1744     }
1745     ctx->worker_running = true;
1746     log_debug("Worker thread started successfully");
1747   }
1748 
1749   // Lock to update final state flags atomically
1750   mutex_lock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1751   ctx->running = true;
1752   ctx->sample_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1753   mutex_unlock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1754   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1755 }

void
  audio_terminate_portaudio_final (void )


Terminate PortAudio and free all device resources. This must be
    called to actually free device structures allocated by ALSA/libpulse. It
    should be called AFTER all audio contexts are destroyed and session cleanup
    is complete.

93       err = Pa_Terminate();
94     });
95

98 }

2038   // Validate batch_count
2039   if (batch->batch_count == 0) {
2040     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count cannot be zero");
2041   }
2042 
2043   // Check for reasonable max (256 frames per batch is very generous)
2044   if (batch->batch_count > 256) {
2045     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count too large (batch_count=%u, max=256)", batch->batch_count);
2046   }
2047 
2048   // Validate channels (1=mono, 2=stereo, max 8 for multi-channel)
2049   if (batch->channels == 0 || batch->channels > 8) {
2050     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid channel count (channels=%u, valid=1-8)", batch->channels);
2051   }
2052 
2053   // Validate sample rate
2054   if (!audio_is_supported_sample_rate(batch->sample_rate)) {
2055     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unsupported sample rate (sample_rate=%u)", batch->sample_rate);
2056   }
2057 
2058   // Check for reasonable sample counts
2059   if (batch->total_samples == 0) {
2060     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch has zero samples");
2061   }
2062 
2063   // Each batch typically has samples_per_frame worth of samples
2064   // For 48kHz at 20ms per frame: 48000 * 0.02 = 960 samples per frame
2065   // With max 256 frames, that's up to ~245k samples per batch
2066   if (batch->total_samples > 1000000) {
2067     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch sample count suspiciously large (total_samples=%u)",
2068                      batch->total_samples);
2069   }
2070 
2071   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2072 }

void
  resample_linear (const float * src, size_t src_samples, float * dst, size_t
  dst_samples, double src_rate, double dst_rate)


Simple linear interpolation resampler. Resamples from src_rate to
    dst_rate using linear interpolation.




Parameters


src Source samples at src_rate


src_samples Number of source samples


dst Destination buffer at dst_rate


dst_samples Number of destination samples to produce


src_rate Source sample rate (e.g., 48000)


dst_rate Destination sample rate (e.g., 44100)


Definition at line 542 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


543                                       {
544   if (src_samples == 0 || dst_samples == 0) {
545     SAFE_MEMSET(dst, dst_samples * sizeof(float), 0, dst_samples * sizeof(float));
546     return;
547   }
548 
549   double ratio = src_rate / dst_rate;
550 
551   for (size_t i = 0; i < dst_samples; i++) {
552     double src_pos = (double)i * ratio;
553     size_t idx0 = (size_t)src_pos;
554     size_t idx1 = idx0 + 1;
555     double frac = src_pos - (double)idx0;
556 
557     // Clamp indices to valid range
558     if (idx0 >= src_samples)
559       idx0 = src_samples - 1;
560     if (idx1 >= src_samples)
561       idx1 = src_samples - 1;
562 
563     // Linear interpolation
564     dst[i] = (float)((1.0 - frac) * src[idx0] + frac * src[idx1]);
565   }
566 }
